items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Critical Progress Told ShareFile Customers to Pull the Plug on Their Servers Her
id: 92cdb033-d568-566b-af9b-210be4e23bd5
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2023-24489 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2023-24489
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/12
modified: 2026/07/12
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2023-24489 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-12T21:03:42Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2023-24489", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2023-24489 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:9664947; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-12, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Intelligence confirmed: Item is listed in CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities; CVSS score 9.8 (Critical); EPSS score 95.080 (very high exploitation probability)

confidence_factors.signal: Item is listed in CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities

items.recommended_sla_action: EMERGENCY PATCH — Active exploitation confirmed or KEV listed
items.apex_ai_summary: [P1] CVE / Vulnerability - moderate severity (Risk 5.9/10). EPSS 95.08% exploitation probability (30-day forecast). MITRE ATT&CK mapped: Phishing (1 technique). Actor attribution: CDB-UNATTR-CVE. AI confidence: LOW (35%) - limited source data available.
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Low Security Vulnerability High EPSS CVE-2026-44383
id: ea7e4e9d-4ebf-5c66-9185-eb5a2e8c481b
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-44383 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: LOW | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44383
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/12
modified: 2026/07/12
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: low
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-44383 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-12T21:03:42Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-44383", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Medium Remote Code Execution High EPSS CVE-2026-13768
id: 8df590db-b8a8-501d-9215-59f81a35c17e
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-13768 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: MEDIUM | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-13768
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/12
modified: 2026/07/12
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: medium
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-13768 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-12T21:03:42Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-13768", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-13768 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:5534005; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-12, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: High confidence based on: CVSS score 10.0 (Critical); EPSS score 56.000 (very high exploitation probability)
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window

description: |

references:

    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-0288
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/12
modified: 2026/07/12
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access

level: medium
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-0288 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-12T21:03:42Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-0288", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc

items.title: File Browser: Command Injection via Authentication Hook Shell Substitution (Pre-Authentication RCE)
items.description:
## Overview

The Hook Authentication feature in File Browser allows administrators to delegate login verification to an external shell command. User-supplied credentials (username and password) are interpolated into this command string using `os.Expand` without sanitization. An **unauthenticated remote attacker** can inject shell metacharacters in the username or password field at the login screen, causing the server to execute arbitrary OS commands before any authentication takes place. This is a **critical pre-authentication RCE**.

## Affected Location

- **File:** `auth/hook.go`
- **Function:** `HookAuth.RunCommand`

## CVSS v4.0

| Metric | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Attack Vector (AV) | Network (N) | Exploitable via the login endpoint over HTTP from any network |
| Attack Complexity (AC) | Low (L) | Single crafted HTTP request; no preparation needed |
| Attack Requirements (AT) | None (N) | No race condition or special timing required |
| Privileges Required (PR) | **Non
items.recommended_sla_action: URGENT PATCH — Critical severity, deploy fix within 72 hours
items.apex_ai_summary: [CRITICAL] Vulnerability advisory. Risk score 9.0/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.
